title: poa pratensis subsp. agassizensis
intro: Poa pratensis subsp. agassizensis, also known as Agassiz's bluegrass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is known to have various health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Agassiz's bluegrass include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat skin conditions and wounds. However, more research is needed to fully understand the effectiveness and potential side effects of using Poa pratensis subsp. agassizensis for medicinal purposes.
